result.summary: Found several visual issues, mostly related to clipping/overflow and inconsistent styling. The page is usable overall, but some elements look unintentionally cut off or overlapped.

issues.description: Multiple items are clipped at the right edge of the viewport, including the top category pill labeled “Hot Ga...” and the third game cards in each row. This makes the page look horizontally overflowed or improperly sized.
issues.suggestion: Ensure horizontal carousels have clear scroll behavior and intentional partial-card peeking, or adjust card/container widths so content does not appear accidentally cut off.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the game content near the bottom of the screen. The lower game cards are partially hidden behind the tab bar.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the scrollable content so the final row can scroll fully above the fixed navigation.

issues.description: The active “Casino” tab in the bottom navigation appears as a default underlined text link, which looks inconsistent with the other nav items and slightly unfinished.
issues.suggestion: Use a custom active-state style instead of browser-default link underlining, such as a color change, icon highlight, or controlled underline treatment.

issues.description: One game tile displays a negative jackpot/value: “-$6,266.13”. This looks suspicious and may be a data or formatting error.
issues.suggestion: Verify the source value and formatting logic for jackpot/amount overlays to prevent negative values from displaying unless intentionally supported.
